summary: SAP — field type mapping for German locale (DECIMAL/NUMC/CHAR)
description_md:
As a workspace-admin, I want the SAP S/4 connector to correctly map German locale field types (DECIMAL, NUMC, CHAR) so that data extracted from our German tenant's SAP data source displays and validates correctly without encoding or formatting errors.

**Background**:
The SAP S/4 Connector currently applies US locale defaults for numeric and character field mappings, causing misalignment when importing customer and material master data from German SAP instances. This results in decimal separator issues (comma vs period) and incorrect character truncation for NUMC and CHAR fields, affecting downstream PII tagging and audit log accuracy.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
1. When connecting to a German locale SAP S/4 data source, DECIMAL fields must use comma (,) as the decimal separator and preserve up to 34 digits with correct sign handling.
2. NUMC fields from the SAP data source must be imported as zero-padded strings without conversion to numeric types, preserving leading zeros for material numbers and customer IDs.
3. CHAR fields must respect the defined SAP field length (up to 255 characters) and apply correct code page (UTF-8) conversion to prevent mojibake in special German characters (Ä, Ö, Ü, ß).
4. The workspace-admin can verify correct field type mapping through the Connector Health Monitor, which displays source-to-target type mappings and sample data transformations for the configured SAP connection.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Testing completed with German SAP test tenant. All three field types (DECIMAL, NUMC, CHAR) now map correctly. Connector Health Monitor shows proper type transformations.
